metadata.description: Agent-era engineering skills for Claude Code

plugins.name: engineering
plugins.description: Ten gated, agent-era engineering skills as one suite — spanning the software lifecycle: requirements (groundwork), architecture (load-bearing), engineering process (flightline), testing (assay), deployment & operations (stationkeeping), maintenance & evolution (husbandry); plus four cross-cutting lenses: the feedback surface (gauge), code craft (plumb), and the security pair — shield (aegis) and spear (gungnir). Each is a multi-stage gated flow, tuned for a world where agents write much of the code. Installs every skill under the /engineering: prefix. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: distributed
plugins.description: Engineer distributed systems for correctness under partial failure — the realities a single-machine programmer (and an agent) get wrong by default. First skill: holdfast (the distributed-correctness lens — the third state where a remote call may leave you NOT KNOWING it ran, idempotency & delivery semantics, retries with backoff+jitter and circuit breakers, ordering by causality not the wall clock, replication without losing data, choosing a consistency model knowing its price — CAP/PACELC, consensus and FLP — partitioning for scale with a key that fits the access pattern, and tolerating faults as the default (failure models, redundancy across independent fault domains, bulkheads and circuit breakers, chaos engineering), over 'don't distribute until you must'). A foundation cut plus replication, consensus, sharding, and fault tolerance (frame · communication · ordering · replication · consensus · sharding · fault-tolerance); distributed transactions & coordination is the one forthcoming stage. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.
